pr-evidence/guard-scheduler-root-facades.gpt55_review_summary: Bernoulli the 3rd found an order-sensitive first version. The test was refactored to isolate fresh facade assertions in a subprocess and keep post-import checks to __all__/star-import behavior. Bernoulli re-reviewed and approved.
scope: Test-only public facade guardrail; no runtime behavior, package exports, or compatibility shim behavior changed.
verification: poetry run pytest tests/test_public_package.py::test_scheduler_compatibility_module_stays_out_of_root_facades tests/test_scheduler.py tests/test_project_governance.py::test_document_package_readme_lists_public_modules_and_console_scripts tests/test_project_governance.py::test_readme_remaining_work_keeps_serving_boundary_explicit -q; PYTHONPATH=src poetry run pytest -q tests/test_scheduler.py::test_scheduler_namespace_remains_compatible tests/test_public_package.py::test_scheduler_compatibility_module_stays_out_of_root_facades; poetry run pytest tests/test_public_package.py tests/test_project_governance.py tests/test_scheduler.py -q; poetry run pytest -q; poetry check; git diff --check; poetry run python -m compileall -q src tests
what_changed: Added a public-surface guardrail proving the scheduler compatibility module remains importable by module path but is not advertised by the document_kv_cache or cachet root facades.
pr-evidence/guard-scheduler-root-facades.why: The V1 serving boundary requires integration with vLLM/SGLang rather than a proprietary scheduler; the legacy scheduler shim should remain compatibility-only and stay out of the public root API.
